seed: batch-04 P4 / spec §9.3; analogy then boundary (Ped-3 boundary variant)
passage: A qubit in superposition is often compared to a spinning coin: while it spins, it is neither heads nor tails until it lands. The analogy helps at first, but it breaks down. Measurement of a qubit is not simply 'looking' at a coin, and entanglement — where two qubits share a state — has no spinning-coin counterpart at all. The lesson is as much where the analogy fails as where it holds.
